executive_summary: Your printing substrates and specialty materials are exposed to a cluster of geopolitical risks across three distinct regions—Nordic/Russian proximity (Stora Enso), US-China trade tensions (IP Sun, Porton), and South African infrastructure stress (Sappi)—each requiring different mitigation timing. More urgent is your EMS and battery supply chain concentration in China, where Smoore's high-risk rating combined with unexplained stock decline (-4.5%) and critical BAT exposure warrants immediate supplier health outreach; EVE Energy and Huizhou BYD show similar patterns of geopolitical exposure compounded by recent equity volatility. ITC (India, high BAT exposure) and Tae Young (South Korea) face regional military tensions that are less immediately volatile but merit contingency mapping. The two US-based equity declines (SWM, Texas Instruments) lack confirmed causes—monitor this week, but they're lower priority unless correlated with supply announcements. Recommend: (1) emergency calls to Smoore and your China EMS/battery suppliers to confirm production continuity and inventory buffers, (2) stress-test substitution pathways for Stora Enso and IP Sun, (3) flag the South African and India tier-1 risks for quarterly scenario planning rather than immediate action.
